summary: Frontend-only landing page testing for fns.expert. All 55 critical UI/integration checks passed across desktop (1280x800) and mobile (390x800). Verified: site-header, full nav, fullscreen 2026 burger menu (open/close on click + close on nav item click), featured testimonial with real photo from fns.expert/assets/images/1.jpg, real-clients section with 9 review photos, lightbox modal (open/next/prev/close), 5 pricing tiers with correct CTAs, final CTA, contact rows, sticky CTAs (desktop+mobile), anchor sections (#features/#how/#pricing/#types/#testimonials/#faq), mobile pricing horizontal snap-scroll (scrollWidth=1480 vs clientWidth=390). All CTA hrefs verified without external navigation.
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: review_request mentions data-testid='sticky-cta' but actual implementation uses 'sticky-cta-desktop' (desktop) and 'mobile-bottom-cta' (mobile). Both work correctly — only naming convention differs from spec. Consider aligning testids for consistency.
Minor: Hero section uses data-testid='hero-secondary-cta' as in-page anchor (#how) which is correct UX, but spec didn't define its target — verified it points to #how (correct).
Code quality: Header.jsx is 293 lines — within limit but approaching split threshold if more features added.
Testimonials.jsx (323 lines) is well-structured but lightbox could be extracted into its own component for reusability.
All href verifications done via attribute reads (no external navigation to crm.fns.expert) as instructed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Landing is fully static (no backend calls). All data-testids from spec are implemented correctly except sticky-cta (split into sticky-cta-desktop / mobile-bottom-cta — intentional, both work). Photos load from external fns.expert CDN (confirmed src attribute = https://fns.expert/assets/images/1.jpg-587x715.jpg etc.). Lightbox uses fixed z-[60], properly stacked over header z-50. Burger menu locks body scroll, closes on Escape and on nav item click. Mobile pricing uses max-md:overflow-x-auto + snap-x-mobile class for horizontal scrolling (verified scrollWidth>clientWidth). No console errors detected.
